name: Nuvation Bio Inc.
business: Nuvation Bio Inc. is a global oncology company that develops and commercializes novel small molecule therapies for cancer. Its only approved product, IBTROZI (taletrectinib), is indicated for the treatment of adult patients with locally advanced or metastatic ROS1-positive non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) in the U.S., and it has out-licensed taletrectinib to partners in Japan, China, and Europe. The company also has a clinical-stage pipeline including safusidenib for IDH1-mutant glioma and preclinical drug-drug conjugate candidates.

exposures.sentence: We commercially launched IBTROZI in the U.S. in June 2025, following its approval by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) on June 11, 2025 for the treatment of adult patients with locally advanced or metastatic ROS1-positive (ROS1+) non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC).
Advancing the ongoing clinical development of safusidenib and continued evaluation of preclinical candidates from our drug-drug conjugate (DDC) platform.

catalysts.event: FDA approval of IBTROZI (taletrectinib) for ROS1+ NSCLC
catalysts.sentence: On June 11, following Priority Review and Breakthrough Therapy Designations for both TKI-naïve and TKI-pretreated disease, the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) approved IBTROZI for the treatment of adult patients with locally advanced or metastatic ROS1+ NSCLC.

catalysts.event: First patient enrolled in TRUST-IV Phase 3 study for adjuvant NSCLC
catalysts.sentence: In September 2025, we announced our first patient was enrolled in a global Phase 3, placebo-controlled study known as TRUST-IV, to evaluate taletrectinib for the adjuvant treatment of patients with resected ROS1+ early-stage NSCLC.

catalysts.event: Publication of positive Phase 2 safusidenib results in Neuro-Oncology
catalysts.sentence: On November 8, 2025, positive results from a Phase 2 study of safusidenib in Japanese patients with chemotherapy- and radiotherapy-naïve grade 2 IDH1-mutant gliomas were published in the online journal of Neuro-Oncology.

catalysts.event: Partnership with Eisai for commercialization in Europe and other territories
catalysts.sentence: In January 2026, we announced a partnership with Eisai Co., Ltd. (Eisai) to commercialize taletrectinib in Europe and other territories outside the U.S., China and Japan.

catalysts.event: Planned MAA submission to EMA for taletrectinib in Europe
catalysts.sentence: In partnership with Eisai, we expect to submit a Marketing Authorization Application (MAA) to the European Medicines Agency (EMA) in the first half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Matured median DOR from pooled TRUST-I and TRUST-II studies (August 2025 cutoff)
catalysts.sentence: However, based on a more recent August 2025 data cutoff, we announced that IBTROZI's median DOR in the pooled TRUST-I and TRUST-II studies had matured to 50 months.

tamClaims.claim: More than one million people globally are diagnosed with NSCLC annually.
tamClaims.sentence: Each year, more than one million people globally are diagnosed with NSCLC, the most common form of lung cancer.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 2% of NSCLC patients have ROS1+ disease.
tamClaims.sentence: It is estimated that approximately 2% of patients with NSCLC have ROS1+ disease (Lin et al 2017; Zhang et al 2019).

tamClaims.claim: Up to 35% of newly diagnosed metastatic ROS1+ NSCLC patients have brain metastases; up to 55% after progression.
tamClaims.sentence: Up to 35% of patients newly diagnosed with metastatic ROS1+ NSCLC have tumors that spread to their brain, increasing up to 55% for those whose cancer has progressed following initial treatment (Ou et al 2019).

tamClaims.claim: Incidence of IDH-mutant glioma in the U.S. is approximately 2,500 new cases per year.
tamClaims.sentence: The Central Brain Tumor Registry of the United States estimated that the incidence of IDH-mutant glioma in the United States in 2018-2021 to be approximately 2,500 new cases per year, with approximately half of such patients classified as low-grade and the other half as high-grade.
